summary: DHH shares early buzz around Singlefile, a web-based personal library management service he was involved with. The post highlights mentions from Hotwired Webmonkey and a #3 ranking on Daypop's top searches. He frames the growing attention as evidence that a 'silent launch' strategy can build organic momentum. The post is extremely brief, serving mainly as a link roundup celebrating early traction.
key_points: Singlefile gained unsolicited coverage from Hotwired Webmonkey, a prominent early-2000s web development publication
The service reached #3 on Daypop, an early blog/news search engine's trending list
DHH advocates for 'silent launch' as a strategy that builds organic word-of-mouth momentum
money_quotes: The silent launch builds even more thunder.
key_insight: A quiet, confident product launch can generate more authentic buzz than a splashy announcement.
